page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g6a.4xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated vCPUs and 16 physical cores utilizing the x86_64 AMD EPYC 7H12 processor, running at a clock speed of 2.6 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or integrated GPU hardware, relying entirely on network-attached storage options. Network connectivity is defined by a baseline bandwidth of 5 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate a clear distinction between single-core and multi-core capabilities. The single-core CPU performance measured via stress-ng is positioned in the bottom 10%, reflecting a lower single-threaded execution speed. However, multi-core CPU performance rises to the middle 50% average tier, supported by the 16 dedicated physical cores. Memory bandwidth tests reveal a similar divergence: cached small-block read operations perform at an average level of 802.27 GB/sec, whereas uncached large-block read and write operations fall into the bottom 25% weak tier at 30.52 GB/sec and 15.5 GB/sec respectively. Other operations, such as Redis SET, static web serving, and Passmark database operations, consistently register average performance.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ecs.g6a.4xlarge offers a balanced resource density profile, providing dedicated physical cores without the premium associated with specialized compute or memory-optimized hardware classes. The lack of bundled local storage and GPUs reduces baseline resource overhead. This instance is appropriate for general-purpose workloads that benefit from multi-threaded execution but do not require high single-core speeds or intensive local disk I/O. Suitable workloads include static web serving, Redis caching, and standard database operations.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g6a.4xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 16 dedicated physical cores on an x86_64 AMD EPYC 7H12 processor running at 2.6 GHz, paired with 64.0 GB of system memory. Operating on the KVM hypervisor, this instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, utilizing a 5 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark data shows a contrast between poor single-core CPU performance and average multi-core execution. Uncached memory bandwidth is relatively weak, though cached small-block reads and multi-threaded decompression align with mid-range standards. Given its balanced resource density, this instance is suited for general-purpose workloads such as static web serving, Redis caching, and standard database operations that leverage multi-core processing without requiring high single-thread speeds.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g6a.4xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 16 dedicated AMD EPYC cores, 64 GB RAM, and 5 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering average multi-core and database performance.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g6a.4xlarge features 16 dedicated AMD EPYC cores and 64 GB RAM, offering balanced general-purpose multi-core performance.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose instance featuring dedicated AMD EPYC cores and a 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio for multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated AMD EPYC 7H12 physical cores running at 2.6 GHz
64.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 4.0 GB per core
5 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with KVM hypervisor integration
Average multi-core CPU, Redis, and database benchmark performance
Tradeoffs include lower single-core speeds and weak uncached memory bandwidth
Best suited for static web serving, Redis caching, and standard databases
